page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This ARM64-based instance features 1 dedicated physical core with 1 thread, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include any GPU hardware, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks show that the single-core CPU capability is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1493.49, placing it in the average tier. However, because of its single-core configuration, multi-core performance metrics are low, matching the single-core score. Memory bandwidth is limited, measuring 19.34 GB/sec for large-block reads and 9.33 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware AES, though overall Passmark CPU encryption and database scores fall into the lower performance tiers.

The primary tradeoff of the m6gd.medium is its single-core limitation and low baseline network bandwidth, which restricts its utility for parallel processing or high-throughput network tasks. However, the inclusion of a local NVMe SSD provides fast local storage access, making it a cost-efficient option for lightweight, disk-I/O sensitive workloads. It is suitable for small-scale web serving, basic database operations, lightweight caching, and development or testing environments that do not require high multi-threaded compute power.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 core running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory and a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. While its single-core stress-ng performance of 1493.49 sits in the average tier, its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single-vCPU constraint. The instance lacks a GPU and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. It offers a cost-efficient hardware profile for workloads that benefit from local SSD storage but do not require heavy parallel processing. Suitable workloads include lightweight web serving, small database operations, caching, and development environments.

tagline: A single-core ARM64 instance featuring dedicated CPU allocation and bundled local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight, disk-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 59 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core CPU performance with hardware AES cryptography support
Low multi-core and memory bandwidth performance due to single-vCPU design
Well-suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments
